MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
DAVID B. NEWDORF, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, (415)554-3892, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
NORMAN NEWHOUSE, ESQ., (650)365-8534, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS SHEA AND PATE. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 BECAUSE DEFENDANTS' MOVING PAPERS DO NOT PROVE THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE HIS CLAIM SINCE DEFENDANTS PRESENT NO EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE THAT COULD ESTABLISH AN INTERFERENCE WITH HIS CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHTS. PLAINTIFF MUST SEEK LEAVE OF COURT IF HE WISHES TO AMEND HIS COMPLAINT. THE COURT ORDERED MR. NEWDORF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)/PM

Motion To Compel Objection Free Responses From Plaintiff Raymond Miller, And Request For Sanction
Off Calendar Mar-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Daniel Zaheer, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: CCSF
No other appearance.
Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendants City And County Of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Objection Free Responses From Plaintiff is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Plaintiff's failure to respond timely was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $500, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
The motion was timely filed. Counsel are advised that the court days are counted from the time of filing and notice and the time for mailing is added at the end of the court days. [See Barefield v Washington Mutual Bank (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 299.]
The court has read and considered Plaintiff's opposition which has been received by Defendant, but which has not been filed. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Amened Notice Of Motion And Motion To Discovery Individual Police Officers' Personnel File
Off Calendar Jan-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Raymond J. Miller's Motion To Discover Individual Police Officers' Personnel File Information is granted as to the personnel records of Officers Coleman and Rightmire. The motion is denied as to Sgt. Richard Pate, Sgt. Shea, Officer Fung, Officer Calaunan, and Officer Johnson as there are no facts supporting a finding of police misconduct or good cause as to those officers.
Defendant is ordered to produce the personnel files of Officers Coleman and Rightmire for in camera review for allegations of excessive force in the past five years. Production of relevant complaints shall be limited to contact information of complainants and witnesses. Any production shall be subject to a confidentiality order limiting use to this litigation only. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
